issue_title: Unrecognized attribute in target gives error that doesn't squelch stacktrace
issue_body:
I ran 'pants' the other day and got an exception that was difficult to read.

```
Exception message: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'resources'
```

There are a few of problems I noticed:

 - The stack trace was not suppressed as I expected it to be (I didn't have --print-exception-stacktrace enable)
 - The name of the BUILD file with the error is buried in the middle of the exception
 - The overall impression of the error makes this look like a bug in pants itself, not invalid BUILD file semantics.

The actual output looked something like this.

```
~/Src/Pants master * devpants goal compile src/python/pants
*** Running pants in dev mode from /Users/zundel/Src/pants/src/python/pants/bin/pants_exe.py ***
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/zundel/Src/pants/src/python/pants/base/build_graph.py", line 341, in target_addressable_to_target
    **addressable.kwargs)
  File "/Users/zundel/Src/pants/src/python/pants/backend/core/targets/dependencies.py", line 33, in __init__
    super(Dependencies, self).__init__(payload=EmptyPayload(), *args, **kwargs)
TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'resources'
ERROR:pants.base.build_graph:Failed to instantiate Target with type <class 'pants.backend.core.targets.dependencies.Dependencies'> with name "pants" at address BuildFileAddress(/Users/zundel/Src/pants/src/python/pants/BUILD, pants)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/zundel/Src/pants/src/python/pants/base/build_graph.py", line 341, in target_addressable_to_target
    **addressable.kwargs)
  File "/Users/zundel/Src/pants/src/python/pants/backend/core/targets/dependencies.py", line 33, in __init__
    super(Dependencies, self).__init__(payload=EmptyPayload(), *args, **kwargs)
TypeError: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'resources'

Exception message: __init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'resources'
```

Here is a change that reproduces the problem:

```
 git diff
diff --git a/src/python/pants/BUILD b/src/python/pants/BUILD
index 325ae1f..e9eaef6 100644
--- a/src/python/pants/BUILD
+++ b/src/python/pants/BUILD
@@ -3,6 +3,7 @@
 
 target(
   name = 'pants',
+  resources = [],
   dependencies = [
     'src/python/pants/bin:pants',
   ],
```

  I saw some code in jar_publish that surrounds `Target.get_all_targets()` with a catch of (TypeError, ImportError, SyntaxError) in [jar_publish.py](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/jar_publish.py#L421)  but I think this code is out of date (there is not Target.get_all_targets() method!) , and catching that exception might already obscure a real bug in pants, I wouldn't want to replicate it. 

Maybe we could just update the exception handler in build_graph.py to handle TypeError() with extra care?

Another thought I had is that we could add `**kwargs` to `Target.__init__()`  and then explicitly look for extra keywords and formulate a more helpful error message.   To keep from obscuring a bug in Pants source code, maybe we could inspect the Target instance and  to see if this target has attributes that will tell us it came from a BUILD file, then raise a different exception.   That's not very pretty (but neither is the error message we get today.)  Unfortunately, I didn't see anything obvious to tell us at that level.

issue_title: Better estimation for what to upload when remoting CAS misses
issue_body:
[This code](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/afaf19876e08165f7e3af66b45a8f6a0d33aa347/src/rust/engine/fs/src/store.rs#L223-L293) is called when a remote execution server tells us that it was missing some blobs in its content-addressed storage.

The `Vec<Digest>` it receives may contain any mix of digests of `Files`, and digests of `Directories`.

`Directories` are pure metadata; binary-serialized protocol buffers of lists of `Files` and `Directories` which are in a named directory.

If the remote side is missing a `Directory`, it cannot tell us which `File`s or `Directories` nested in it are also missing.

This code currently attempts to upload all transitive children of any missing `Directory`. This may be wasteful - in the extreme case, all of the `Directory`'s children are already present on the remote side, and only the top-level Directory metadata is missing.

The `remote::ByteStore` in that same file already has an address of a CAS server, which implements the [ContentAddressableStorageService gRPC API](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/afaf19876e08165f7e3af66b45a8f6a0d33aa347/3rdparty/protobuf/googleapis/google/devtools/remoteexecution/v1test/remote_execution.proto#L156-L230) which has a function to return the missing digests from a list of digests.

We should modify our `ensure_remote_has_recursive` function to use some heuristics to decide whether to all of the files, or whether to ask the server which files it needs, and just upload those. Some inputs that come to mind for this heuristic:
 * Number of files to maybe upload
 * Size of files in bytes to maybe upload
 * Other data we may have (e.g. we could keep a list of "recently uploaded files" to model what we believe the server probably has)

I recommend tacking this in stages:
1. Give the `remote::ByteStore` a `cas_client` field as well as a `bytestream_client` field (which is currently named `client`)
2. Write a new method on `remote::ByteStore` which calls the `FindMissingBlobs` API. Include some tests :)
-- Cut a PR for the above --
3. Modify `ensure_remote_has_recursive` to implement some heuristic for determining whether to filter its expanded digests list using the new `FindMissingBlobs` method.

issue_title: AWS::Lambda::Permission for log-group subscription filter fails to apply lambda:InvokeFunction action on CloudWatch logs
issue_body: Hi team, ## Description: I'm trying to automate my stack by doing: - Create Function - Create ApiGateway - Create LogGroup ------------------------> need AWS::Lambda::Permission here (for complete automation) - Create a subscription filter for the logGroup to stream the logs to a different lambda. ## Problem Statement: When I try the 4th step inside the template, the stack fails by saying: ## Template.yml: ``` AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09 Transform: AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31 Parameters: executionRole: Type: String Default: execution_role s3Bucket: Type: String Default: functions_bucket s3ObjectKey: Type: String functionName: Type: String Default: poc_lambda logCollectorLambdaName: Type: String Default: cloudwatch-log-collector-lambda stageName: Type: String Default: myTestStage aliasName: Type: String Default: test Resources: ApiGatewayApi: Type: AWS::Serverless::Api Properties: StageName: !Sub ${stageName} DefinitionBody: swagger: "2.0" info: title: !Sub ${functionName} paths: "/commerce": get: responses: {} x-amazon-apigateway-integration: uri: !Sub "arn:aws:apigateway:${AWS::Region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:function:${functionName}:${aliasName}/invocations" httpMethod: POST type: aws_proxy Commerce: Type: AWS::Serverless::Function Properties: FunctionName: !Ref functionName AutoPublishAlias: !Ref aliasName Description: "poc lambda" Handler: src/index.handler Runtime: nodejs6.10 CodeUri: Bucket: !Ref s3Bucket Key: !Ref s3ObjectKey Events: Catalog: Type: Api Properties: RestApiId: !Ref ApiGatewayApi Method: GET Path: /commerce MemorySize: 128 Timeout: 3 Role: !Sub "arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/${executionRole}" Tags: name: !Ref functionName AutoPublishAlias: !Ref aliasName LogGroup: Type: AWS::Logs::LogGroup Properties: LogGroupName: !Sub "/aws/lambda/${functionName}" RetentionInDays: 14 LogGroupLambdaInvokePermission: Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission Properties: FunctionName: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:function:${functionName}" Action: "lambda:InvokeFunction" Principal: !Sub "logs.${AWS::Region}.amazonaws.com" SourceArn: !Sub "arn:aws:logs:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:log-group:/aws/lambda/${functionName}:*" SourceAccount: Ref: "AWS::AccountId" DependsOn: "LogGroup" LogsSubscriptionFilter: Type: AWS::Logs::SubscriptionFilter Properties: LogGroupName: !Sub "/aws/lambda/${functionName}" FilterPattern: "" DestinationArn: !Sub "arn:aws:lambda:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:function:${logCollectorLambdaName}" DependsOn: "LogGroupLambdaInvokePermission" ``` ## Research: I read a lot of blogs that implement this design - 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/SubscriptionFilters.html 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-lambda-permission.html#cfn-lambda-permission-sourceaccount _which is a subset of_ https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/API_AddPermission.html#SSS-AddPermission-request-SourceArn 3. https://medium.com/@Prestomation/serverless-slackops-real-time-linking-to-logs-when-your-lambda-function-crashes-edbd8e0353e1 4. http://blog.davidablack.net/2016/08/17/a-log-reflector-for-aws-lambda/ 5. https://rockytech.blogspot.com/2017/02/massively-integrating-aws-cloudwatch.html went through some of the open/closed issues in SAM - - https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/issues/144, https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/issues/257 and few others (that talk about AWS::Lambda::Permission implementation). and also did more than 50 Cloudformation deployments with different combinations to make this work. ## Workaround: The only workaround for having the subscription filter in the template and still make this work, is to: 1. **Remove** the subscription filter on the first deployment. This will only create the logGroup (because that's in the template). 2. After this, you can add the subscription filter manually by navigating to Cloudwatch and putting the subscription on the logGroup. **AWS will create the AWS::Lambda::Permission for you**, which you cannot view (?). 3. Once the subscription filter is in place, you can add back the code in the template that creates the subscription filter. 4. On all the subsequent runs, *even if the stack is cleared out and recreated*, the subscription filter permission is intact with AWS and you don't get the error message anymore. In short, you won't need the `AWS::Lambda::Permission` in the template anymore, because AWS has that automatically created for you. ## It would be very helpful if anyone can point out the issue (if any) in the template, OR assure me that this is a bug, and that I am missing something obvious. Thanks, Ravi Pandey.

issue_title: Data Item I048/020, Target Report Descriptor: XPP missing
issue_body:
At least in version 1.21 of the CAT048 specification the Data Item I048/020, Target Report Descriptor contains the XPP bit in the first extend. So

```xml
<Bits from="7" to="6">
	<BitsShortName>spare</BitsShortName>
	<BitsName>spare bits set to 0</BitsName>
	<BitsConst>0</BitsConst>                       
</Bits>
```

should be

```xml
<Bits bit="7">
	<BitsShortName>spare</BitsShortName>
	<BitsName>spare bit set to 0</BitsName>
	<BitsConst>0</BitsConst>                       
</Bits>
<Bits bit="6">
	<BitsShortName>XPP</BitsShortName>
	<BitsName>XPP</BitsName>
	<BitsValue val="0">No X-Pulse present</BitsValue>
	<BitsValue val="1">X-Pulse present</BitsValue>
</Bits>
```

issue_title: Persistent BGP and BGP Flow Spec
issue_body:
Hello, Thomas!

On ENOG9 we have really awesome report about DDoS mitigation for Russia's greatest IX (http://www.enog.org/presentations/enog-9/44-msk-ix-enog-9-kazan.pdf) and they described very interesting topic about BGP persistency.

Case is really simple. We have huge attack to the network with multiple vectors (udp, tcp) and we have mitigated significant part of attack with Flow Spec.

But in some time attack changes vector (for example, to ICMP) and kill whole network. In this time we will lost BGP sessions and already loaded flow spec rules will be dropped from all routers/firewalls. Really worst case. 

But this feature could be solved with some sort of timeout until drop announces from disconnected peer. I.e. we need persistency for BGP and BGP Flow SPEC NLRI.

BGP persistency become supported in recent release of Cisco IOS: http://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/routers/asr9000/software/asr9k_r5-2/general/release/notes/reln_522a9k.html#concept_122333ADDC0B491FAA28A05045B18510 

And my voice for BGP persistency support for ExaBGP. Some nice articles there http://www.slideshare.net/bduvivie/bgp-flowspec-phase-2 and there https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-uttaro-idr-bgp-persistence/

Thank you!

issue_title: Progressive config files override
issue_body:
Make CWD config partially overrides user-local config, which partially overrides the system-wide config files.

For example, if `/usr/local/share/dwave/dwave.conf` has:
```
[defaults]
endpoint = <production>
client = qpu

[prod]
token = <token>
```

and `~/.config/dwave/dwave.conf` has:
```
[alpha]
endpoint = <alpha>
token = <token>
```

Then the user should be able to run `dwave ping --profile prod` **and** `dwave ping --profile alpha`.

issue_title: More modular feature finding
issue_body:
As discussed in #239 and recently in #244, I would like to open a discussion on making feature finding more modular and hopefully more flexible and readable. I had a look at the code and we could breakdown locate in the following steps:

    <parameter checks>
    invert(raw_image)  # I would prefer to put this in a process_func and remove it from locate
    image = preprocess(raw_image, noise_size, smoothing_size, threshold)
    coords = local_maxima(image, radius, percentile, margin)
    filter(coords, minmass, maxsize)
    refine(raw_image, coords, separation, max_iterations, engine)  #possibly with characterize
    filter(coords, minmass, minsize, topn)
    characterize(raw_image, image, coords, radius, threshold)

To make the API user-friendly, we can work with `function`, `_function` combinations respectively including and not including the parameter checks.

I wonder what the performance loss would be if we would remove characterization from `refine` and write a new (possible numba) `characterize`.

What is the best place to make the transition `ndarray` -> `DataFrame`?

issue_title: Fix leaky `_child_services` property on Service class
issue_labels: [{'id': 631592722, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw2MzE1OTI3MjI=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/Good%20First%20Issue', 'name': 'Good First Issue', 'color': '0e8a16', 'default': False}, {'id': 889922027, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw4ODk5MjIwMjc=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/comp:%20p2p', 'name': 'comp: p2p', 'color': 'bfd4f2', 'default': False}, {'id': 983629570, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5ODM2Mjk1NzA=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/effort:%20E1%20hours', 'name': 'effort: E1 hours', 'color': 'bfe5bf', 'default': False}, {'id': 983627565, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5ODM2Mjc1NjU=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/prio:%20P2%20required', 'name': 'prio: P2 required', 'color': 'f7c6c7', 'default': False}, {'id': 630871939, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw2MzA4NzE5Mzk=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': 'eeeeee', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/0ff7bc0f820250d7f15e804bc09870776ea34eb5/p2p/service.py#L21

The `Service` class has list set as a class property.  This will cause all instances of the `Service` class to share this list which is not the desired behavior.

cc @cburgdorf (this is a common mistake in python, classes should rarely have mutable data structures set at the class level)

### How can it be fixed

Remove the assignment of the list from the class level into the `__init__` constructor.

```python
class Service:
    _child_services: List['BaseService']

    def __init__(self, ...):
        ...
        self._child_services = []
```

issue_title: Dev docs
issue_body:
Conda doesn't use ReatTheDoc which is fine - I assume there are good reasons for it, however the conda docs seem to be missing some features as compared to RTD.

Specifically the docs don't seem to be tied to any particular version of `conda`. issue_title: deactivate env fails on OS X for PyStan (uses new compilers)
issue_body:
I'm attempting to create an environment in which to use PyStan. PyStan creates Python C/C++ modules on-the-fly and so uses the Anaconda compilers. I'm trying to keep my base env clean in terms of paths to standard compilers (like clang); the PyStan env changes `clang` to point to Anaconda's compiler rather than Apple's.

I have Miniconda (Py-3) newly installed, and I created the env this way:

`conda create -n py36stan python=3.6 anaconda pystan`

Activating it appears to work fine:

```
$ source activate py36stan
INFO: activate_clang_osx-64.sh made the following environmental changes:
+AR=/Users/tester/miniconda3/envs/py36stan/bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-ar
+AS=/Users/tester/miniconda3/envs/py36stan/bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-as
+CC=x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-clang
+CFLAGS=-march=core2 -mtune=haswell -mssse3 -ftree-vectorize -fPIC -fPIE -fstack-protector-strong -O2 -pipe
+CHECKSYMS=/Users/tester/miniconda3/envs/py36stan/bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-checksyms
+CLANG=/Users/tester/miniconda3/envs/py36stan/bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-clang
+CODESIGN_ALLOCATE=/Users/tester/miniconda3/envs/py36stan/bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-codesign_allocate
+CONDA_BUILD_SYSROOT=/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/MacOSX10.13.sdk
...
```

Deactivating fails:

```
$ source deactivate
ERROR: This cross-compiler package contains no program /bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-ar
ERROR: deactivate_clang_osx-64.sh failed, see above for details
ERROR: This cross-compiler package contains no program /bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-clang++
ERROR: deactivate_clangxx_osx-64.sh failed, see above for details

$ echo $AR
/Users/tester/miniconda3/envs/py36stan/bin/x86_64-apple-darwin13.4.0-a
```

Note the last; $AR did not exist in the base env; it is created for the new one and removing it is evidently aborted when `deactivate` fails.

I find the error messages unclear. I don't know what package they refer to. I also don't know if they refer to a problem in the new py36stan env or in the base env.

I realize Anaconda 5 has new, arch-specific compilers, implementing a kind of cross-compilation capability:  [Utilizing the new compilers...](https://www.anaconda.com/blog/developer-blog/utilizing-the-new-compilers-in-anaconda-distribution-5/?lang=en-us). I don't fully understand when they need to be used and when/whether the Apple compilers mentioned on that page need to be installed. I do know that `clang_osx-64` and `clang_osx-64` are installed as PyStan dependencies, and that they put a bunch of `x86_64-apple-darwin...` tools in the /bin directory of the env. I tried installing these two clang packages in my base env before creating the PyStan env, but the deactivate failure still happens.

Is there something I'm neglecting to do with `conda`, or is this is a bug?

      ==> file at https://anaconda.org/contact/report
  * commands under 'conda build':
      ==> file at https://github.com/conda/conda-build
  * commands under 'conda env':
      ==> please file it here!
  * all other conda commands that start with 'conda':
      ==> please file it here!

If you continue on, and especially if you are submitting a bug report,

   **please include all requested information below.**

If a maintainer determines the information is required to understand 
your issue, and if it is not provided, your issue may be
closed automatically.

-->

**I'm submitting a...**
  - [ ] bug report
  - [x] feature request


The basic requirement is to allow more than one package names for the same package. For example, there is a package called 'x-c' and it's github repo goes by the name 'libx' or 'xlib'. I want the users to get the same package, regardless of whether they do `conda install x-c` or `conda install xlib` or `conda install libx`. Right now, the simple way to do this is to have the recipe create multiple outputs and make one depend on the other, but that means two/three tarballs per release, which is inconvenient. It'd be great if one could specify something like:
```yaml
package: 
  name: {{ name }} 
  aliases:
      - {{ alias1 }}
      - {{ alias2 }}
  version: {{ version }}
```
This would inject into the metadata extra information, which would then be parsed by conda and make the solver think that there are three packages: name, alias1 and alias2 all with the same version.

issue_title: channel UNC path encoding errors on windows
issue_body:
I have some channel specifications for UNC file paths on windows that stopped working in conda 4.2.11, but did work in 4.2.9 and earlier

The conda command with channel specification followed this style
conda install -c file://\\machine\shared_folder\path\conda  my_package

Channels specified in condarc were also affected and caused a bug when parsing the condarc file (via calling a conda command).

Is there another preferred style for UNC paths on windows?


I see some new channel decoding code in the channel model that is likely the culprit.  
if value.startswith('file:') and on_win:	
	value = value.replace('\\', '/')	
        return Channel.from_url(value)

issue_title: conda env prompt
issue_body:
activate.bat change the PROMPT to show activated conda env, which is nice. However, it has conflict with other software that also tries to set PROMPT, for example, git use prompt to show current branch.

I found on google group the discussion about this:
https://groups.google.com/a/continuum.io/forum/#!topic/conda/C4ll3MeLku0

which suggest an option to not change the PROMPT. 

However if I just change the following line:

set PROMPT=[%CONDA_NEW_NAME%] $P$G
to:
set PROMPT=[%CONDA_NEW_NAME%] %PROMPT%

It adds conda evn while keeping the PROMPT set by git.

I did not test other scenarios. BTW, I'm on Windows with conda version 3.18.8:

issue_title: Sort compounds when loading
Deadlock starting in bcolz 1.0.0
issue_body:
Hello,

We've been having new releases of our application deadlock and we believe we tracked it down to a difference between bcolz 0.12.1 and bcolz 1.0.0.

 Our application is a single-threaded webserver using bcolz to store historical information. When our application deadlocks it becomes completely unresponsive to HTTP requests and uses 0% CPU (which is highly unusual for our webserver). The most unusual thing we do is perform a fork during the initialization step.

We installed bcolz from anaconda's repos. we're on linux 64-bit. the machines are running Ubuntu 14.04. 

I can collect core dumps and stack traces from the deadlocked processes. Attached is one such deadlocked process.

My application is closed source so I cannot share source code or core dumps.

```
#0  pthread_cond_wait@@GLIBC_2.3.2 () at ../nptl/sysdeps/unix/sysv/linux/x86_64/pthread_cond_wait.S:185
185	../nptl/sysdeps/unix/sysv/linux/x86_64/pthread_cond_wait.S: No such file or directory.
(gdb) bt
#0  pthread_cond_wait@@GLIBC_2.3.2 () at ../nptl/sysdeps/unix/sysv/linux/x86_64/pthread_cond_wait.S:185

issue_title: Scoll with scroll_id parameter is broken on ES 6.x
issue_body:
Continuing a scroll and clearing a scroll is broken against the latest master build of Elasticsearch.

The following snippets demonstrates the problems:

```python
import elasticsearch

# force content type header for ES 6.x
class ConfigurableHttpConnection(elasticsearch.Urllib3HttpConnection):
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        super(ConfigurableHttpConnection, self).__init__(**kwargs)
        self.headers.update({"Content-Type": "application/json"})


es = elasticsearch.Elasticsearch(hosts=[{"host": "localhost", "port": 9200}], connection_class=ConfigurableHttpConnection)

# first page
r = es.search(body={
    "query": {
        "match_all": {}
    }
}, scroll="10s")

scroll_id=r["_scroll_id"]

try:
	# continue scrolling - will fail with HTTP 400
	es.scroll(scroll_id=scroll_id)
finally:
	# will fail too with HTTP 400
	es.clear_scroll(scroll_id=scroll_id)
```

Workaround: Explicitly define the request body, i.e instead of:

```python
es.scroll(scroll_id=scroll_id)
```

 we can use

```python
es.scroll(body={"scroll_id": self.scroll_id, "scroll": "10s"})
```

and instead of

```python
es.scroll(scroll_id=scroll_id)
```

we can use

```python
es.clear_scroll(body={"scroll_id": [scroll_id]})
```

issue_title: az monitor metrics list should support aggregation type argument
issue_body:
The azure metrics API supports multiple aggregation types e.g. average, total, minimum, maximum, etc (for more info see [here](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/monitoring-and-diagnostics/monitoring-rest-api-walkthrough#retrieve-metric-values)). This is passed as an additional argument in the odata query string e.g. (aggregationType eq 'Total').

The `az monitor metrics list` command does not accept this argument, which means you'll only get the default aggregation. This can block scenarios such as trying to find out the total number of Azure Functions executions you have via the CLI.

Environment info:
```
azure-cli (2.0.16)
monitor (0.0.9)
```

```python
In my scenario we are querying the metadata endpoint which calls a method (_populate_from_metadata_endpoint) in msrestazure/azure_cloud.py. This appears to be hardcoded to not pass verify=False to retrieve the endpoints. Editing this to pass this verify=False gets Ansible running.

issue_title: Job template wont create task if i add resource files collection
Replace CONCAT with MSCONCAT
issue_body:
There is a design limitation in concat due to passing all of the paths in on the URI which can cause failure when concatenating a large number of files. Until this is fixed in a new WEBHDFS protocol, please use the MSCONCAT op code and pass in the paths in the body of the request as an application/octet-stream using the following format for the stream in the body:
sources=<path1>,<path2>,<path3>...,<pathN>

To see more information about this API you can see the swagger specification for it here:
https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/master/arm-datalake-store/filesystem/2015-10-01-preview/swagger/filesystem.json#L335

Additionally, for further optimizations for concat please see issue: #49

each bone that is to have physics has a 'bone tag' parented to it, and a shild shape

I purpose a build in system that does all this under the hood but also computes the friction during the move maybe using substeps?

however she will not walk forward only by playing an animation - I have to combine the animation with a forward sero motion.

issue_title: Issue in btKinematicCharacterController::stepDown() needs review
issue_body:
The function `btKinematicCharacterController::stepDown()` in `btKinematicCharacterController.cpp` has the following code at line 567:

    if (m_ghostObject->hasContactResponse() && (callback.hasHit() &&
            needsCollision(m_ghostObject, callback.m_hitCollisionObject)) || runonce == true)

There are parentheses around the second `&&` but no parentheses to make it explicit that the branch will *always* be taken when `runonce` is `true`, which makes me suspect this code does not do what it should.

The code used to look like this:

    if ((callback.hasHit() || runonce == true) && m_ghostObject->hasContactResponse() &&
             needsCollision(m_ghostObject, callback.m_hitCollisionObject))

issue_title: S3 Put Object Failed with aiohttp==1.1.6
issue_body:
Hi,

I found a problem using `aiobotocore` with the latest version of `aiohttp`. Apparently, [the latest aiohttp](https://github.com/KeepSafe/aiohttp/compare/v1.1.5...master) depends on [`yarl==0.8.1`](https://github.com/aio-libs/yarl/compare/v0.7.1...v0.8.1) or greater. If I downgrade it to `aiohttp==1.1.5` with `yarl==0.7.1`, the problem doesn't occur.

## Problem

If I'm using a parenthesis on `Key` value, it will throw an exception.

## Example

```python
import asyncio, aiobotocore
loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
session = aiobotocore.get_session(loop=loop)
s3_client = session.create_client('s3')
s3_bucket = "test-bucket-name"
s3_path = "test-(key).txt"
loop.run_until_complete(s3_client.put_object(Bucket=s3_bucket,Key=s3_path, Body="test"))
```

The code throws:

```
botocore.exceptions.ClientError: An error occurred (SignatureDoesNotMatch) when calling the PutObject operation: The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided. Check your key and signing method.
```

However, the following code works properly:

```python
import asyncio, aiobotocore
loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
session = aiobotocore.get_session(loop=loop)
s3_client = session.create_client('s3')
s3_bucket = "test-bucket-name"
s3_path = "test-key.txt"
loop.run_until_complete(s3_client.put_object(Bucket=s3_bucket,Key=s3_path, Body="test"))
```

Tested with `aiobotocore==0.0.6` and `botocore==1.4.73`

issue_title: gstpocketsphinx.c: can't set hmm property when default hmm directory doesn't exist
issue_body:
I'm trying to specify -hmm via gst properties (I'm using gst1.0), but it's failing because my machine doesn't have a model in the default -hmm value's path.

In gst_pocketsphinx_init, if the directory specified by -hmm (or the default -hmm value) doesn't exist, cmd_ln_parse_r returns NULL [1], which causes the `ps->ps == NULL` check to raise an error.  This is all done before properties have been set.  So I assume the `ps_init` call and NULL check should be done after the properties are set.

[1] `0:00:03.679641618  1635      0x1c10aa0 ERROR           pocketsphinx :0:: ERROR: "acmod.c", line 80: Acoustic model definition is not specified either with -mdef option or with -hmm`

issue_title: Can't run 0.3.1 samples in virtualenv - wrong urllib3 version
issue_body:
Steps to reproduce:

1. Start with a fresh Raspbian 2017-04-10 image
1. `sudo apt-get install -y python3-virtualenv portaudio19-dev libffi-dev libssl-dev`
1. `python3 -m virtualenv -p python3 env`
1. `env/bin/pip install --upgrade pip`
1. `env/bin/pip install --upgrade google-assistant-sdk[samples]`
1. `env/bin/googlesamples-assistant-pushtotalk`

Fails with:

`RuntimeError: Requests dependency 'urllib3' must be version >= 1.21.1, < 1.22!`

This appears to be because [google-assistant-grpc](https://github.com/googlesamples/assistant-sdk-python/blob/master/google-assistant-sdk/googlesamples/assistant/grpc/requirements.txt) pulls in urlli3b 1.20. I don't know what path the deps follow to lead to a version of requests that requires urllib3 1.21.

issue_title: Make nbgrader install a real subcommand
issue_body: Now that the install command is a proper app, we might as well make it a proper subcommand of nbgrader so users can see it in the command line documentation and run it like the other commands.

issue_title: meta-c copies the entire line if nothing is selected (with no way to disable)
issue_body:
I use a accessibility tool called `voicecode` to allow me to dictate my computer use. One of the tricks that it uses is to send a `meta-c` and check the clipboard to a program to determine if the user has anything selected. Jupyter notebooks is configured so that if nothing is selected this keyboard shortcut copies the entire line. This breaks some of the functionality.

I've tried modifying `IPython.keyboard_manager.edit_shortcuts.add_shortcut('meta-c', "jupyter-notebook:ignore")`, but have only succeeded in disabling copying by shortcut altogether. Is there a way to disable line copy in the event that nothing is selected?

issue_title: Printing slides seems to have broken page formatting (nbconvert then ?print-pdf)
issue_body: **Preamble** This is a tricky one to explain since it involves several systems, but I'll try my best, have included steps to reproduce and screenshots. I hope there's a simple fix because I've tried most combinations I can think of, and found no joy. I think the issue is somewhere in Jupyter's nbconvert utility, since reveal.js seems to work when I hand-carve html presentations. **The aim** Create a pdf version of a slidedeck, for printing and sharing etc **Issue** The PDF formatting is broken, and multiple slides appear on one sheet of paper, not respecting page breaks. **Background: my setup** 1. Basics: `python 3.5, jupyter 4.1.0, reveal.js 3.3.0, OSX 10.10.5` 2. Create new virtualenv using `conda env create --file test_presentation.yml` (which contains the following) name: test_presentation dependencies: - python=3.5 - jupyter - pip **Create a very basic presentation for testing** 1. Fire up jupyter notebooks `$> jupyter notebook` and create a slideshow using the slideshow cell toolbar with the following structure (slideshow cell toolbar indicates in parens): ``` # Slide 1 (This is a slide) Freetext # Slide 2 (this is a slide) Freetext ## SubSlide 2a (this is a sub-slide) Freetext # Slide 3 (this is a slide) Freetext ### Fragment 3a (this is a fragment) Freetext ### Fragment 3b (this is a fragment) Freetext # Slide End (this is a slide) Freetext ``` **Convert slides and present in-browser as normal** 1. Use nbconvert to create slides and serve jupyter nbconvert --to slides --post serve test_presentation.ipynb 2. File `test_presentation.slides.html` is created and served locally. Open it in Chrome (`Version 50.0.2661.75 (64-bit) (OSX)`) 3. Notice the slides render as expected: and, _crucially_ they are separated as expected, one slide per page - see screenshot (I've escape-zoomed out to show multiple slides at once) **Try to view print-pdf version and discover this formatting issue** 1. Add print-pdf to the end of the url `http://127.0.0.1:8000/TestPresentation.slides.html?print-pdf` 2. Now I find that the slides do NOT respect any page breaks nor boundaries. They appear to be smushed up together sometimes, 3. Also the final slide is missing from even this smushed up version 4. Naturally, when I use Chrome to print to PDF, (as described here https://github.com/hakimel/reveal.js#pdf-export) the bad formatting is preserved and printed 5. I've also tried without success to locally install reveal js: encounter same issue 6. Ive also tried to mess with the displayport / window size using phantom js e.g. `phantomjs reveal.js/plugin/print-pdf/print-pdf.js "http://localhost:8000/test_presentation.slides.html?print-pdf" test_presentation.pdf 1280x800` Anyone have any ideas? Thanks!

issue_title: Adding a "output_updated.OutputArea" event
issue_body:
The ``display`` function provides a very convenient way of updating output using the ``display_id`` and the resultant display handle. The updates take a very different codepath to regular ``display_data`` and ``execute_result`` messages however, and therefore do not provide a way to subscribe to a general event which makes the whole mime bundle and the OutputArea available.

In JupyterLab this is possible because the ``renderModel`` method of any registered custom mime renderers is given the whole mime bundle. It would be quite convenient to have an equivalent mechanism, which could easily be implemented by adding an ``output_updated``  event for ``OutputArea``. If this idea sounds reasonable I'd be happy to put together a PR.

issue_title: Use default value when value is None
issue_body:
Using the default keyword is convenient.
```python
class Person(Model):
    name = StringType(default='Guido')

p = Person() 
print(p.name) 
>>> 'Guido'
```
However, if the value gets set to None, the default isn't triggered.

```python
p = Person({'name': None}) 
print(p.name)
>>>'None'
```
This makes it hard to abstract away the instantiation of a model.
Ideally the default value should always be set when the value is None.

Use case:
When getting a request from the web that you'd like to parse, the request will look something like this:
`{"name": None}`.  If you dump this into your Schematics Model, the default will never get set.  

In this case, subclassing the type and overriding the `convert`, `to_native`, or `to_primitive` methods doesn't work, because they never get called when a `None` value is passed in.

issue_title: Multiline fields containing braces not working
issue_body:
I think the problem appears only when a field has two consecutive lines ending in `},`.  Here's my test case:

```python
import bibtexparser

bibtex_string = """@ARTICLE{Cesar2013,
  author = {{A},
            B. and {C},
            D.},
  title = {An amazing title},
}
"""

print(bibtexparser.loads(bibtex_string).entries[0]['author'])
```
The expected output is `{A}, B. and {C}, D.`; the actual output is just `{A}, B. and {C`.  If I remove either of the newlines in the `author` field, I get the expected output.

I'm running the current git version of this package on python 3.6.2.

issue_title: Partial Signatures
issue_body:
When a file has a certificate file but is not signed with it, apksigner shows some special message:
```
Verifies
Verified using v1 scheme (JAR signing): true
Verified using v2 scheme (APK Signature Scheme v2): false
Number of signers: 1
Signer #1 certificate DN: CN=?????????, OU=?????????, O=?????????, L=??, ST=???, C=CHN
Signer #1 certificate SHA-256 digest: 7c3fa539c685fac904700f63d7dbfddb7b501384860cdd3102992b5134c90b3e
Signer #1 certificate SHA-1 digest: 13cbb87072055dd6a5ee87a31a5fd3081fb6c573
Signer #1 key algorithm: DSA
Signer #1 key size (bits): 1024
Signer #1 public key SHA-256 digest: 748f43aa57cd8bf73f7ae2b7dae52f70247cd1c8f8a3111e7acef1abe68d8626
Signer #1 public key SHA-1 digest: 682eead4097308f8737556961ccd65d128ac1dfc
WARNING: Partial JAR signature. Found: META-INF/CERT.DSA, missing: META-INF/CERT.SF
WARNING: META-INF/CERT.DSA not protected by signature. Unauthorized modifications to this JAR entry will not be detected. Delete or move the entry outside of META-INF/.
```

But we detect two signatures:
```
In [1]: a.get_signature_names()
Out[1]: ['META-INF/ANDROIDA.DSA', 'META-INF/CERT.DSA']
```

we should check if a SF file exists with the same name - probably we would also require to check if the file is correctly signed.

In this case, the CERT.DSA is actually the same certificate as in ANDROIDA.DSA, it was probably added later. But there might be cases where another file is added.

issue_title: This codebase should contain an f5-common base exception that is the parent of all specific exceptions raised in this library
issue_body: For consumers of this library, it would be nice to be able to import f5.common.exceptions in order to catch errors appropriately. The consumer would then not need to know how requests are performed in this library or how exceptions can occur. The consumer would be able to catch all exceptions with the parent f5-common.exceptions.base_exception or catch something more specific with f5-common.exceptions.ObjectUpdateException.

issue_title: Switch to using external k5test
issue_body: Currently, we use a vendored k5test.  I've extracted k5test (https://github.com/pythongssapi/k5test) and placed it on PyPI, so it should be fine to switch to using that and remove our vendored version.

issue_title: Why do we need uk and uk_UA?
issue_body:
Hello!

Could you, please, explain to me why there are two files for Ukrainian translations: `uk` and `uk_UA`. And the main issue is that there are not all translations in the `uk_UA` file: https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/blob/master/src/oscar/locale/uk_UA/LC_MESSAGES/django.po#L4763

There is not even `uk_UA` language in django settings: https://github.com/django/django/blob/master/django/conf/global_settings.py#L135

So why do we need uk_UA in django-oscar? And, why django chose it, but not `uk` file?

Also, there are files for Russian: `ru` and `ru_RU`. And `ru` is not translated completely but `ru_RU` is.

issue_title: Possible Performance Regression from 2.0.7 to 2.1-RC1 on Windows Server 2016 w/ Hyper-V Role
issue_body:
Hello .NET Core Team and Community,

I have a very interesting performance issue for you here.  I have produced some code that demonstrates a regressing difference in performance when switching from the .NET Core 2.0.x runtime to 2.1-RC1 on my Windows Server 2016 machine that I am using as a Hyper-V server.  Please note that I am also using Visual Studio 2017 15.7.1 in producing my output here.

To start, this issue has context that is most recently rooted in a Benchmark.NET issue that you [can view here](https://github.com/dotnet/BenchmarkDotNet/issues/739).  I have created a series of five benchmarks using the exact same underlying test code.  Each have a slight modification that produce different results in 2.0.7 when run on my Windows Server host machine (note: the host machine and not a VM instance).  You can see an [overview of this information here](https://github.com/dotnet/BenchmarkDotNet/issues/739#issuecomment-386811232).

I took the code that is in the 3rd example, and by simply rebuilding it and running it in the 2.1 runtime, it produced slower results.  I have [created this 2.1 branch here](https://github.com/Mike-EEE/BenchmarkDotNet.InProcess/tree/TheWeird_03a) for your convenience.

(Please do note that the results of each scenario are found in the README.md of their respective branches.)

Additionally, with a small change, I was once again able to produce the faster result times as seen in 2.0.7 but within the 2.1 runtime.  I have published this [branch here](https://github.com/Mike-EEE/BenchmarkDotNet.InProcess/tree/TheWeird_03b).  This is consistent with the bizarre behavior referenced in the five earlier examples, but seen here in a different form (or configuration, for lack of a better term) in 2.1.

I feel it's worthy to note that I believe that this is a gremlin of a problem that I have been chasing in .NET Core tooling since 1.1.  I have encountered the most weird of circumstances around this behavior while trying to benchmark code using Benchmark.NET.  You can see previous iterations of my previous, more complicated attempts at wrestling this beast here for your reference:
https://github.com/dotnet/BenchmarkDotNet/issues/330
https://github.com/dotnet/BenchmarkDotNet/issues/433

Now that I have been able to show a (regressing) discrepancy in performance between 2.0.7 and 2.1 RC1 with a simple recompile, I believe this could be due to a tooling/SDK problem that has been around for some time now.  Fortunately, I have been able to capture it using a very simple project this time and am more than happy to share it now in hopes to finally tracking down this very weird issue. 😄

issue_title: Still having backslash parsing errors in apache config file (0.4.2)
issue_body:
I've seen all the old bugs about Apache config file parsing errors with regards to backslashes and updates to the augeas lenses, but I'm still getting this error when trying to run 'letsencrypt-auto --apache':

`The error was: PluginError(('There has been an error in parsing the file (%s): %s', u'/etc/apache2/sites-enabled/piwigo.conf', u'Syntax error'),)`

caused by these two RewriteCond lines (which have backslashes):

```
RewriteCond %{HTTP:Content-Disposition} \.php [NC]
RewriteCond %{THE_REQUEST} ^[A-Z]{3,9}\ /.+/trackback/?\ HTTP/ [NC]
```

If I comment those out, the parsing works (and then I run into the "Failed to connect to host for DVSNI challenge" problem, which I'm still working on.

I even tried updating to the 1.4.0 versions of augeas-lenses and libaugeas0 (I had 1.3.0 on Wily, 1.4.0 is coming with Xenial) but that didn't help either. I'm guessing it's safe to just leave those lines commented out to get past the parsing and then turn them back on once I'm done with the letsencrypt process/setup?

issue_title: Remove [testing] setuptools extras
issue_body:
In #1665, I moved mock from the packages' `install_requires` to their `tests_require`, which allowed me to switch from an explicit invocation of nose in tox.ini to `python setup.py test`. However, to minimize disruption late in the release cycle and keep my PR on target, I did only what was necessary. This ticket is about finishing the job.

Let's…
* Move nose from the `testing` extra to `tests_require` (if we in fact require it).
* Move auxiliary tools like coverage and pep8 from the testing extra to tox, which is what calls them.
* Remove the testing extra altogether if it ends up being empty.

issue_title: Can't tell if Kafka broker is unreachable
issue_body:
I have a python app that uses the confluent Kafka client, which in turn relies on librdkafka. We are using librdkafka version 0.9.1.

I am trying to set up a Kafka producer (and later, a consumer). The actual addresses or FQDNs of the brokers is, of course, configurable. From the point of view of my app, one of the first things I am interested in before I can start my work is, did I successfully connect to a broker? I create a Producer, and I produce(), but what if the broker is down or unreachable, or the user simply entered garbage in the broker list? I find that flush() hangs for an unacceptably long time in these cases.

What I'd like is visibility and control. After I create the Producer, I want to be able to ask it, did you succeed in establishing a connection with at least one broker? I see that in the C code in librdkafka-master, there is the rd_kafka_broker_any(rk, RD_KAFKA_BROKER_STATE_UP) routine. Is there anything like this through the python API? I might want to be able to walk the broker list and ask the connection state of each. My app might want to take different actions depending on our failure to connect to the broker cluster. The closest I think I can some is a loop, asking for len(my_producer) after I produce(), and waiting for it to go fro m 1 to 0. Is there nothing better?

Is there something clever I could do with callbacks that would solve my problems?

Thanks,

-John Gregg

Here is a portion of my dummy test script:

def my_kafka_cb(err, msg):
    print("my_kafka_cb, err {}, msg {}".format(str(err), str(msg)))

conf = {'bootstrap.servers': '1.2.3.4',
        'group.id': 'mygroup',
        'debug': 'all',
        'socket.timeout.ms': 5000,
        'socket.max.fails': 1,
        'session.timeout.ms': 3000}

kafka_producer = Producer(**conf)

print("just created, len {}, about to poll 4.5".format(len(kafka_producer)))
print("************************** poll returned {}".format(kafka_producer.poll(4.5)))
kafka_producer.produce("Fred_topic", "Fred the dog went for a walk".encode('utf-8'), on_delivery=my_kafka_cb)

issue_title: How is deserialization affected by only and partial?
issue_body:
I was hoping to get some clarification on the differences between ```only``` and ```partial``` with respect to deserialization. (This question is similar to #509 which addresses serialization using the same fields.) Based on the docs and the response to that issue, I interpreted it to mean that ```only``` applies to serialization and ```partial``` applies to deserialization. However, in testing it looks like ```only``` also affects deserialization. What am I missing?
```python
from marshmallow import Schema, fields

class MySchema(Schema):
    foo = fields.Field(required=True)
    bar = fields.Field(required=True)

only_schema = MySchema(only=('foo', ))
partial_schema = MySchema(partial=True)

print(only_schema.load({'foo': 42, 'bar': 24}))
print(only_schema.load({'foo': 42}))
print(partial_schema.load({'foo': 42, 'bar': 24}))
print(partial_schema.load({'foo': 42}))
```
Output:
```python
UnmarshalResult(data={u'foo': 42}, errors={})
UnmarshalResult(data={u'foo': 42}, errors={})
UnmarshalResult(data={'foo': 42, 'bar': 24}, errors={})
UnmarshalResult(data={'foo': 42}, errors={})
```
I expected deserialization using ```only_schema``` to result in an error similar to the example below. 
```python
MySchema().load({'foo':42})
```
Output:
```python
UnmarshalResult(data={'foo': 42}, errors={'bar': [u'Missing data for required field.']})
```
Ultimately, I'm trying to get away with using the same schema for (de)serialization, but maybe that's not the ideal way to use marshmallow.

issue_title: Never calls obj_create when it's a ToManyField
issue_body:
I don't know if it's happening in another related field, but consider this code

```python
class CelebrationResource(ModelResource):
    owner = fields.ForeignKey('users.api.UserResource', 'owner', full=True, readonly=True)
    events = fields.ToManyField('apps.events.api.EventResource', 'events', full=True, full_detail=is_full_event, full_list=is_full_event, null=True, related_name='celebration')

    class Meta:
        queryset = Celebration.objects.all().distinct('id')
        authentication = OAuth20Authentication()
        authorization = CelebrationAuthorization()
        resource_name = 'events/celebration'
        always_return_data = True


class EventResource(ModelResource):
    celebration = fields.ForeignKey(CelebrationResource, 'celebration', full_list=is_full_celebration, full=True, full_detail=is_full_celebration)

    class Meta:
        queryset = Event.objects.all().distinct('id')
        authentication = OAuth20Authentication()
        authorization = EventAuthorization()
        resource_name = 'events/event'
        always_return_data = True

    def obj_create(self, bundle, **kwargs):
        # do something
        bundle = super(EventResource, self).obj_create(bundle, **kwargs)
        # do domething
        return bundle
```

As you can see, I have two resources and there are related, Celebration has many Events inside, so it has a ToManyField and Event has one Celebration, that's why it has a Foreign key.

Nevertheless, when I'm creating a Celebration with its events by using a single json, obj_create is never called (I actually checked out tastypie code and didn't find any obj_create method when m2m_hydrate. This means the "do something" parts in my code are not executing. And Event is not being creating the same way it creates if I use Event resource uri.

For example I'm creating a Celebration object using a json like this

```json
{
    "name": "my celebration",
    "events": [
        {
            "name": "my event",
        }
    ]
}

Unfortunately the bug does not trigger when my application runs in `strace` or `gdb` suggesting that undefined behaviour in the allocator is the root cause. The above dump is from a core file which I'd rather like to not provide as it's 50 gigs in size. I'm currently re-running my experiment under valgrind to gather more information about this. Any quick ideas for fixes?

Best,
Julian

issue_title: Low priority feature request: make `get-value` work with Pareto fronts in SMTLib mode
issue_body:
This is a _low-priority_ request.

As discussed in #1001, the `get-value` based interface currently doesn't work for retrieving model-values for Pareto fronts. The current work around is to use:

```
    (set-option :opt.print_model true)
```

to bring out those models. This is less than ideal as it does not allow for precise control of how model values are queried. It is also quite different from how boxed objectives can be retrieved where a `model-index` can be passed, as discussed in https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/issues/955. 

It would be nice if Pareto models can be queried via calls to `get-value`; if at all possible. Perhaps via a `(next-pareto-front)` like directive to move to the next front till we exhaust the pareto fronts.

issue_title: Resut of bit-blast tactic for formula with nested quantifiers is incorrect
issue_body:
I have been trying to use `bit-blast` tactic with `:blast-quant true` to bit-blast some quantified bit-vector formulas. However, the tactic yields some formulas that are not-equivalent to the input ones. 

For example, I suppose that the result of the following two calls should be the same:
```
(assert (forall ((x (_ BitVec 4)) (y (_ BitVec 4)))
                (= (bvadd x y) #x0))))

(apply (using-params bit-blast :blast-quant true))
``` 
```
(assert (forall ((x (_ BitVec 4)))
                (forall ((y (_ BitVec 4)))
                        (= (bvadd x y) #x0))))

(apply (using-params bit-blast :blast-quant true))
```
However, the respective results are different:
```
(goals
(goal
  (forall ((x.0 Bool)
           (x.1 Bool)
           (x.2 Bool)
           (x.3 Bool)
           (y.0 Bool)
           (y.1 Bool)
           (y.2 Bool)
           (y.3 Bool))
    (let ((a!1 (= (not x.2) (= y.2 (or (not x.3) (not y.3)))))
          (a!2 (not (or (not x.2) (or (not x.3) (not y.3)))))
          (a!3 (not (or (not y.2) (or (not x.3) (not y.3))))))
    (let ((a!4 (or (not (or (not x.2) (not y.2))) a!2 a!3)))
    (let ((a!5 (or (not (or (not x.1) (not y.1)))
                   (not (or (not x.1) (not a!4)))
                   (not (or (not y.1) (not a!4))))))
    (let ((a!6 (or (= (not x.3) y.3)
                   a!1
                   (= (not x.1) (= (not y.1) a!4))
                   (= (not x.0) (= (not y.0) a!5)))))
      (not a!6))))))
  :precision precise :depth 1)
)
```
```
(goals
(goal
  (forall ((x.0 Bool) (x.1 Bool) (x.2 Bool) (x.3 Bool))
    (forall ((y.0 Bool) (y.1 Bool) (y.2 Bool) (y.3 Bool))
      (let ((a!1 (or y.2 (not (or (not y.2) (not y.3))))))
      (let ((a!2 (or y.1 (not (or (not y.1) (not a!1))))))
      (let ((a!3 (or (= (not y.2) (= (not y.2) y.3))
                     (= (not y.1) (= (not y.1) a!1))
                     (= (not y.0) (= (not y.0) a!2)))))
        (not a!3))))))
  :precision precise :depth 1)
)
```
The second result is obviously wrong as it does not contain any `x._` variable.

Unfortunately, merging all quantifiers into one block does not solve my problem because I need to work also with formulas with quantifier alternations.

This behaviour occurs both in the stable release 4.7.1 and the latest git version `adb9a1c7978d236ce1d177c775905079db681e96`.

Thanks for investigating.

issue_title: SEGFAULT libz3
issue_body:
Hello angr community,

I'm here for a rather tough issue I think. And We're not even sure if angr is the right place for this. However, After many days of testing with a segfaulting libz3 during symbolic execution I dumbed it down to a very special case: Run Symexec in a Thread.

The following code dies after arround 11 steps (deterministic):

```
In [8]: INFO    | 2018-04-12 10:09:21,246 | angr.engines.engine | Ticked state: <IRSB from 0x10070310c: 1 sat>
INFO    | 2018-04-12 10:09:21,264 | angr.engines.engine | Ticked state: <IRSB from 0x1007030f8: 1 sat 1 unsat>
INFO    | 2018-04-12 10:09:21,365 | angr.engines.engine | Ticked state: <IRSB from 0x100702fe8: 1 sat 1 unsat>
INFO    | 2018-04-12 10:09:21,396 | angr.engines.engine | Ticked state: <SimProcedure ReturnUnconstrained from 0x100cb7bd4: 1 sat>
INFO    | 2018-04-12 10:09:22,404 | angr.engines.engine | Ticked state: <IRSB from 0x100703104: 1 sat>
INFO    | 2018-04-12 10:09:22,429 | angr.engines.engine | Ticked state: <IRSB from 0x100703028: 1 sat>
INFO    | 2018-04-12 10:09:22,448 | angr.engines.engine | Ticked state: <IRSB from 0x100703118: 1 sat 1 unsat>
INFO    | 2018-04-12 10:09:22,466 | angr.engines.engine | Ticked state: <SimProcedure ReturnUnconstrained from 0x100cb7bd4: 1 sat>
INFO    | 2018-04-12 10:09:22,487 | angr.engines.engine | Ticked state: <SimProcedure ReturnUnconstrained from 0x100cb8444: 1 sat>
INFO    | 2018-04-12 10:09:22,529 | angr.engines.engine | Ticked state: <IRSB from 0x100703124: 1 sat>
INFO    | 2018-04-12 10:09:22,546 | angr.engines.engine | Ticked state: <IRSB from 0x100703118: 1 sat 1 unsat>
Segmentation fault (core dumped)

[ 8661.483092] ipython[22204]: segfault at 0 ip 00007fe45d6715c0 sp 00007fe46611e830 error 4 in libz3.so[7fe45c73f000+11eb000]
```

However, running the exact same code without the Thread - finishes the symbolic execution. Also deterministic. Its not a problem of the sym-exec itself when run from the main-loop. I can not make the binary public here for the community, but could send it to the angr core group, if you provide me with some contacts. 

The first general question would be: Do you know of such behaviour in general? Might it be claripy or is it really z3. It should not be about "Thread safety" as only a single thread executes the symexec. 

```python
import logging
logging.basicConfig()
l = logging.getLogger()
l.setLevel(logging.INFO)

import angr
import threading
from angr.exploration_techniques.explorer import Explorer

backend = "mach-o"
binarypath = ...

proj_options = {'auto_load_libs': False, 'main_opts': {'backend': backend}}
project = angr.Project(binarypath, load_options=proj_options, translation_cache = False)

def target1(project, ):
  NSLOGV_ADDR = 0x100CB6770
  #NSLOGV_TARGET_ADDRS = map(lambda addr: addr[0], filter(lambda addr: addr[1] == NSLOGV_ADDR, ida.callsites))
  
  NSLOGV_CALLSITE_ADDRS = [ 0x100703164, 0x100703408 ]
  l.info("Target list for _NSLogv is {}".format(map(hex, NSLOGV_CALLSITE_ADDRS)))
  
  # Backwards calculated start-addr's for sym-exec from first NSLOGV_CALLSITE_ADDR (0x100703164) 
  NSLOGV_FIRST_TARGET_ADDR = 0x100703164
  NSLOGV_START_ADDRS = [0x10070310C, 0x1007030F8, 0x100702FE8] 
  l.info("Target list for _NSLogv is {}".format(map(hex, NSLOGV_START_ADDRS)))

  # Prepare necessary hooks
  hooklist = [ 0x100CB7BD4, 0x100CB8444 ]
  for hookaddr in hooklist:
      if not project.is_hooked(hookaddr):
          project.hook(hookaddr, angr.SIM_PROCEDURES['stubs']['ReturnUnconstrained'](), kwargs={'return_val': None})

  startpathlist = []
  for curaddress in NSLOGV_START_ADDRS:
      state = project.factory.blank_state(addr=curaddress)        
      startpathlist.append(state)

  # Create simgr
  simgr = project.factory.simgr(startpathlist)

  tech = Explorer(find=NSLOGV_FIRST_TARGET_ADDR,
                      find_stash='found',
                      avoid_stash='avoid',
                      num_find=1)

  simgr.use_technique(tech)
  simgr.run()
  simgr.remove_technique(tech)

t1 = threading.Thread(target = target1, args = (project, ))
t1.start()
```

Greetings,
Andreas

We read that we should consult you first to figure out if there is a better solution than Channel Overrides for our problem.

We are a research team working on autonomous UAV flight in GPS deprived environments.

In order to do that, we need to

1. Arm the drone without GPS (is the "GUIDED_NOGPS" mode the right one for this?)

issue_title: 卖出小于1手的情况
issue_body:
## 1. RQAlpha的版本
2.2.7
## 2. Python的版本
3.6
## 3. 是Windows/Linux/MacOS or others?
Windows
## 4. 您出现问题对应的源码/或者能复现问题的简易代码 以及对应的配置
问题比较简单，ALPHA策略中由于分红送股，导致持仓数小于1手，这些零散的持仓无法凑整也无法卖出，在计算调仓中非常消耗计算。

issue_title: 关于StockCommission中的get_commission的疑问
issue_body:
在rqalpha/mod/rqalpha_mod_sys_simulation/decider/commission.py中的StockCommission类的get_commission方法中用到了self.commission_map,  其计算手续费的代码如下:

```
        commission = self.commission_map[order_id]
        cost_money = trade.last_price * trade.last_quantity * self.rate * self.multiplier
        if cost_money > commission:
            if commission == self.min_commission:
                self.commission_map[order_id] = 0
                return cost_money
            else:
                self.commission_map[order_id] = 0
                return cost_money - commission
        else:
            if commission == self.min_commission:
                self.commission_map[order_id] -= cost_money
                return commission
            else:
                self.commission_map[order_id] -= cost_money
                return 0
```

上述代码中使用了一个self.commission_map, 个人感觉没有意义, 因为一个Order只生成一个Trade, 所以计算手续费可以直接使用如下代码:

```
        cost_money = trade.last_price * trade.last_quantity * self.rate * self.multiplier
        if cost_money > self.min_commission:
            return cost_money
        else:
            return self.min_commission
```

如果一个Order是分多个交易成交的, 那么应该通过该Orde已成交的总数量来计算该Order的手续费, 
而不是分别计算每次Trade的手续费, 
一个Order多次交易的手续费应该以Order为单位、而不是以Trade为单位来计算.

issue_title: Rename myOpts to options in GaudiRun application
issue_body: The name `myOpts` in the `GaudiRun` application is too colloquial and doesn't fit with the general naming in `Ganga`. It should just be renamed to `options`.

issue_title: pip install version not installing latest
issue_body: attempting to install version 2.0.0 to get the latest Facebook SDK versions but even when i do sudo pip install facebook-sdk --upgrade or if i do sudo pip install facebook-sdk==2.0.0 it ALWAYS installs 1.0.0

issue_title: Is it at all possible to use `collection_timestamp` in readonly mode?
issue_body:
Currently, the `collection_timestamp` method fails if the timestamp for a collection is requested on a readonly node and the timestamp doesn't exist. Are there other options besides failure here? Could we maybe fill in a default `TIMESTAMP_UNAVAILABLE` last_modified (maybe `INT_MIN`) and in this way allow calling code to continue to function with a fake but innocuous timestamp that will not cause data loss later?

/cc @bqbn

issue_title: Using Code from PyCWT
issue_body:
There's been quite a few attempts at a CWT code in Python and I guess most have made use of the Torrance and Compo code and the [Matlab](http://noc.ac.uk/marine-data-products/cross-wavelet-wavelet-coherence-toolbox-matlab) Wavelet ToolBox. kPywavelets was the most complete version which is now renamed as [PyCWT](https://github.com/regeirk/pycwt). 

I recall discussing this at EuroSciPy 2014, that it wouldn't be possible to use any of the resulting python code due to the licensing issues but I was able to get permission from the authors to licence, as BSD, the Python code that used their codebases as inspiration within PyCWT. [I think the permissions](https://github.com/nabobalis/pycwt/tree/master/license) I got via email was enough but I am not very knowledgeable about licences. 

I wasn't sure if there are parts of the code that could be submitted here, at least for the extra functionality to be added to the CWT and the inclusion of cross-wavelets. Obviously any code submitted would need to be rewritten to align with the style and quality of the code used here. I was thinking of the significance calculation, power bias reification and cross-wavelet functions among others. Are there any plans to expand the CWT part of this module?

ConnectionBase.edges should be NonNull

We're using `gql2flow` to generate Flow types from the GraphQL schema and found that all of our `Connection` fields are defined with `edges` being nullable. 

According to [`graph-relay-js`](https://github.com/graphql/graphql-relay-js/blob/5927f0becf43740a1f1026c6e960811c4359d342/src/connection/connectiontypes.js) seems that `ConnectionBase.edges` should be defined as `NonNull(List(edge))`

Is there a scenario in which `edges` may be `None`?

issue_title: AsyncIO Executor
issue_body:
I've run into a bug with the AsyncIO Executor when trying to run a query from an application where the event is running. 

I think it's because the executor's implemention of `wait_until_complete` uses `run_until_complete` which starts up the event loop and waits. Since the event loop is already running, it threw an exception when I tried to start it a second time.

issue_title: create integration test template for shells
Implementations *must* raise UnsupportedFormatException on unknown formats
Add diagnostics module in OpenThread
issue_body:
The diagnostics module is a tool for debugging platform hardware manually, and it will also be used during manufacturing process, to verify platform hardware performance.

There will be two kinds of diagnostics features:
- Common diagnostics features
These features are all platform independence, which are based on existing common platform interface defined in _`include/platform/`_.

- Platform specific features
Since OpenThread will be built on various platforms, there will be some platform specific diagnostics features for different platforms, these diagnostics features will be implemented in each platform layer and provide _`diagPlatform()`_ interface for OpenThread diagnostics module.

issue_title: @step_from_factory no longer picks up arbitrary factory class names as step definitions
issue_body:
I upgraded from `0.0.34` to the most recent release and ran into the following behaviour: factories decorated with  `@step_from_factory` now fail to correctly infer the step text from the class name.

I have inherited some feature files with steps like:
```
Given I have nationwide services:
...
```

which has an associated factoryboy factory like:

```
@step_from_factory
class NationwideService(ServiceFactory):
    foo = bar
```

Prior to `0.1.15`, this was working fine. Now, however, I get the following error:
```
NoDefinitionFound: The step r"And I have nationwide services:" is not defined
```

I'm not sure if this change was intentional or not. It appears to have been introduced in 365a59b0cbf3348a1bf5ca5db3d9e0927d3dd3a4. By the looks of that commit, it fixed a bug which caused the decorator to *always* infer the step name from the factory class name, not the model's name (https://github.com/aloetesting/aloe/commit/365a59b0cbf3348a1bf5ca5db3d9e0927d3dd3a4#diff-91332d13b9c03c246990815584252355R106).

Now, since there is a model called `Service`, that gets picked up and preferred over inferring the factory name from the class name of the factory (as per https://github.com/aloetesting/aloe/commit/365a59b0cbf3348a1bf5ca5db3d9e0927d3dd3a4#diff-91332d13b9c03c246990815584252355R108)

As I said, I'm not sure if this changes was intentional or not, but given that the author of those steps and factories is an author of this library, I'm assuming it was an unintentional side effect of that changeset.

issue_title: Data augmentation modifies the original input data(?)
issue_body:
When processing an input mini batch of images with data augmentation (e.g., random blur) during training, are the original images also modified? Normally, I would expect that a copy of each batch is processed at each iteration and the original images are not modified, otherwise, the transformations are accumulated and this is not expected.

Is this the case in tflearn? If it is, then it is very important and should at least be emphasized in the documentation.

I have been doing cross-validation and realized that the validation performance steadily drops at each cross-validation iteration, which was unexpected. Then, I provided the training with a new copy of the data at each CV iteration and the results improved significantly. However this is only a partial solution, if the original training data is overwritten/modified by data augmentation, since it gets accumulated at each epoch.

issue_title: ACMEv2 client includes "status" field in newOrder JWS payload
issue_body:
## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):

Cloned from git:
```
$> git rev-parse HEAD
6b29d159a2f221c3437770bdb43924ee6f953c4b
```

## I ran this command and it produced this output:

`certbot_test --server http://localhost:4001/directory certonly --standalone -d one.wtf --preferred-challenges http-01 `

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:

The JWS payload sent to the `newOrder` endpoint contained two fields not mentioned in modern ACME drafts (e.g. draft-10, draft-11, "ACME v2"): "resource" and "status".

```
{
  "status": "pending", 
  "identifiers": [
    {
      "type": "dns", 
      "value": "one.wtf"
    }
  ], 
  "resource": "new-order"
}
```

The first unexpected field, "resource", is already captured in https://github.com/certbot/certbot/issues/5708

The second unexpected field, "status", is something assigned by the server in the returned order object and not a field that should appear in the client's newOrder request. [Section 7.4 of draft-11](https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-ietf-acme-acme-11#section-7.4) only specifies the following fields for a newOrder request: "identifiers", "notBefore" and "notAfter". Because Let's Encrypt does not support the latter two fields I would expect a newOrder JWS body to look like:
```
{
  "identifiers": [
    {
      "type": "dns", 
      "value": "one.wtf"
    }
  ], 
}
```

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:

with `deploy_hook = "some-command"` in the `/etc/letsencrypt/renewal/example.com.conf` file the hook was unseen.

with the deprecated `renew_hook = "some-command"` in the `/etc/letsencrypt/renewal/example.com.conf` file the hook was seen and properly ignored.

issue_title: Spot existing redirects and respond with caution
issue_body:
A typical complex case would be something like this:

http://redant.com.au/ruby-on-rails-devops/manage-ssl-redirection-in-nginx-using-maps-and-save-the-universe/

In that case we should probably be able to spot a directive like this:
`return 301 http://`... and refuse to try to enable a redirect in such a configuration (or warn/ask the user before trying)

issue_title: [nginx] IPv6 & HTTP 2 support?
issue_body:
Currently the nginx plugin adds:
`listen 443 ssl; # managed by Certbot`

It should add the following for IPv6 support:
```
listen 443 ssl; # managed by Certbot
listen [::]:443 ssl; # managed by Certbot
```

An option for HTTP 2 would be nice too, either by specifying `--http2` flag or justcheck nginx for compatibility.
```
listen 443 ssl http2;
listen [::]:443 ssl http2;
```

issue_title: Should we keep Certbot from pushing expired certs live?
issue_body:
We had a report that due to some other misconfiguration issues, an important website had an outage because Certbot took an old, expired cert it had acquired and made it live.

Should we maybe have some checks that ensure that when Certbot makes a cert live, that cert isn't expired (unless the user uses a --force-expired flag or something like that)?

issue_title: Pymavlink conflicts
issue_body:
Based upon this comment:
https://github.com/ArduPilot/MAVProxy/commit/b704fb901fc87a21b04909ba2811cf86f4d0baa0

After further research, there appears to be some confusion about which mavlink we're supposed to use with MAVProxy.

From Ardupilot's Mavlink (https://github.com/ArduPilot/mavlink/commit/f4ef4fb7bdd43b5f110d81953057126b15c1a58c), `MAV_TYPE_DODECAROTOR` has a value of `28`

However, Lorenz's Mavlink (https://github.com/mavlink/mavlink/commit/bd9b88d6a6014adb11a725ac24e190e152a9f8ec) has  `MAV_TYPE_PARAFOIL` has a value of `28`

Looking at pymavlink on Pypi (https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pymavlink), Version 2.2.6 has  `MAV_TYPE_PARAFOIL` has a value of `28`. So Lorenz's pymavlink is in the Pypi repo. Or there's been some really strange git merging going on between the Ardupilot's and Lorenz's pymavlink.

Having essentially two different pymavlinks is going to make it super confusing and difficult for any GCS's (ie MAVProxy)  out there.

In MAVProxy's case, it will crash if started up using copter SITL and using the Pypi pymavlink.

Should MAVProxy be able to support different implementations of pymavlink? Or we just tell the users to only use the Ardupilot pymavlink?

issue_title: Non Compliant LTI Systems
issue_body:

Looking at the code there is a section for canvas -- LAUNCH_PARAMS_CANVAS

I have a vendor who doesn't want to remove their non-standard parameters:
basiclti_submit
launch_url

Which is causing the launch to fail when I receive it:

lti.launch_params:InvalidLaunchParamError: launch_url is not a valid launch param
File "/opt/nogginllc/learnwell/env3.5/lib/python3.5/site-packages/lti/launch_params.py", line 198, in __setitem__

There are a few ways I can think of to handle these issues:
1. (I hate this idea) Add the non-standard params to the launch_params.py file.
2. (I hate this idea) Monkey patch the launch params
3. (I hate this less but this is a bad idea) Remove validation altogether
4. (Getting Better) Make Launch Parameter validation optional
5. (Best Idea, but not sure easiest way to implement) Allow passing additional allowed parameters at runtime.

I would like to hear thoughts about this

issue_title: possibly track uploads separate from `task_max_age`
issue_body:
In [bug 1394130](https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1394130), we ran out of t2 cpu creds. Often this resulted in killing the task when we were uploading, which crashed scriptworker (supervisorctl restarted).

We may want to track uploads separate from `task_max_age`, which would allow us to upload artifacts with their own timeouts.

Related: #128

issue_title: Increase deposit timeout for Dev Preview
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

With Ropsten having quite long and inconsistent blocktimes we sometimes run into a timeout when depositing (https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/api/python.py#L223-L232). 

## Solution

Increase the timeout time to be 120 seconds rather than 60, in order to increase the changes of avoiding a timeout.


## Tasklist

- [ ] Increase timeout

issue_title: Unclean Matrix shutdown due to long polling sync request
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Matrix uses HTTP long polling (with a default poll timeout of `30s`).
During Raiden shutdown the Matrix transport waits a total of `10s` for all greenlets to terminate.
Depending on the time in the request cycle and the activity the client sees it's possible that the long polling sync request will block longer than `10s` leading to: 
https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden-libs/blob/7ac0d335b76e17c6ca47cd73ba548fe4a9b15d10/raiden_libs/network/matrix/client.py#L200

Solution: keep track of greenlets performing the sync long polling requests and `.kill()` them before calling `.join_all()` on the remaining ones.

issue_title: Only base classes provided in Django 1.11b1
issue_body:
I'm testing a project against Django 1.11b1 and it seems that only the base model is being returned in the resulting ``PolymorphicQuerySet``. Only upon calling ``get_real_instances()`` to I get the actual model instances.

Here is my working:

```python
>>> Action.objects.all()
# 'Action' is the base class
<PolymorphicQuerySet [<Action: Action object>, <Action: Action object>, <Action: Action object>, <Action: Action object>]>
>>> Action.objects.all().get_real_instances()
# Now the resolution works
[<EmailAction: Email toby@example.com>, <EmailAction: Email adam@example.com>, <EmailAction: Email foo@example.com>, <EmailAction: Email bar@example.com>]
```

This seemed to work when testing against Django 1.10.

issue_title: Are factory.django.DjangoModelFactory fixtures in conftest.py available across applications?
issue_body:
https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-django/issues/508

I'm using factory_boy with https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-django.

pytest-django: 3.1.2
factory-boy: 2.8.1  (downgraded due to https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-factoryboy/issues/47#issuecomment-318951957)
django: 1.11.4

Here's my conftest.py:

```python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import factory
import pytest
from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model
from django.core.management import call_command
from pytest_factoryboy import register

class UserFactory(factory.django.DjangoModelFactory):
    class Meta:
        model = get_user_model()

    username = factory.Sequence(lambda n: "user%03d" % n)
    password = factory.Sequence(lambda n: "pass%03d" % n)

register(UserFactory)
```

This makes ``user`` available as a fixture for me. However, it only works in tests in the same application, not project-wide (in different django application test directories).

Am I doing it wrong, then? I'm trying to narrow down if this is a mistake on my end, a bug, a potential feature, or expected behavior.

issue_title: Defunct jupyter notebooks in jenkins tests.
issue_body:
It turns out this is fairly easy to reproduce.

If you build the Ray docker image with

```
cd ray
./build-docker.sh
```

Then start a container

```
docker run -t -i ray-project/deploy:latest /bin/bash
```

First check to see if there are any notebook processes.

```
ps aux | grep jupyter
```

Then run the following in Python.

```python
import subprocess
subprocess.Popen(['jupyter-notebook'])
```

This will fail with a message like

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/opt/conda/bin/jupyter-notebook", line 6, in <module>
    sys.exit(notebook.notebookapp.main())
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/jupyter_core/application.py", line 267, in launch_instance
    return super(JupyterApp, cls).launch_instance(argv=argv, **kwargs)
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/traitlets/config/application.py", line 652, in launch_instance
    app.initialize(argv)
  File "<decorator-gen-7>", line 2, in initialize
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/traitlets/config/application.py", line 87, in catch_config_error
    return method(app, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/notebook/notebookapp.py", line 1071, in initialize
    self.init_webapp()
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/notebook/notebookapp.py", line 919, in init_webapp
    self.http_server.listen(port, self.ip)
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/tornado/tcpserver.py", line 126, in listen
    sockets = bind_sockets(port, address=address)
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/tornado/netutil.py", line 194, in bind_sockets
    sock.bind(sockaddr)
  File "/opt/conda/lib/python2.7/socket.py", line 228, in meth
    return getattr(self._sock,name)(*args)
socket.error: [Errno 99] Cannot assign requested address
```

If you attach to the container in another terminal window (filling in the appropriate value of `CONTAINER_ID`), e.g.,

```
docker exec -t -i CONTAINER_ID /bin/bash
```

and check for jupyter notebooks, you'll see `ps aux | grep jupyter` prints something like

```
root        56  1.8  0.0      0     0 pts/0    Z+   23:36   0:00 [jupyter-noteboo] <defunct>
```

Exiting from the original python process will get rid of the defunct jupyter notebook.

It's possible that in our jenkins tests the python process that starts the jupyter notebook is not exiting properly or something.

cc @shaneknapp

issue_title: Enable object store to spill to disk when memory fills up.
issue_body:
Currently, when an object store fills up, it starts evicting objects in a least-recently-used manner.

**Some question:**
- Should we store objects on disk when we evict them?
- What should we do when the disk fills up?
- How much disk space should it be allowed to use?
- Should we expose a separate API for persisting objects to disk (e.g., `ray.persist`).
