issue_title: Using websockify with NAT for multiple VNC servers across different PCs
issue_body:
Hello,
I have a question regarding usage of websockify with NAT: say that I need to have a publicly available HTTPS server which sits in front of the NAT clients. In this scenario, one NAT client is actually PC with running VNC server and there can be many clients like this. Lets say that adding NAT client(s) does not have to be 'dynamic' (on the fly) - it is desirable, but not mandatory. 

Is it possible (and if - yes, how?) that users from the outside world, sees only HTTPS server (some login page) and depending on selected NAT client (VNC server) be connected to it. I manage to do this using a port from the pool of opened ports in the given range, so external user may connect to VNC behind NAT using websocket. 

My client however is concerned that this is a security breach (due multiple opened ports for websockify to use) and would prefer that we use only one websockify port to achieve this. Plus, that port must not be available from the public network. In short, he would prefer that 443 is used for both HTTPS server (NGINX) and also websockify to target multiple VNC servers behind NAT. Additionally, multiple users can access such system at the same time. I, on the other hand am not convinced that this is possible (at least part considering sharing 443 between HTTPS server and websockify). 

I tried to find a solution on google, but the closest thing I found is to use HTTPS server that targets multiple VNCs by tampering systemd processes - but even in this solution, different ports on HTTPS server were used for different VNC servers (each target VNC server has dedicated VNC port at HTTPS server PC); and number of possible VNC clients is measured in 100s, so extending number of daemons that runs  websockify is not practical (at least I think so), plus, most of the time users are idle and not using a connection.

Thank you all in advance.

Best regards

issue_title: Pass raw spans to exporters
issue_body:
Currently, span objects are converted to an intermediate json format before being sent to an exporter. As a result, there are some stackdriver specific conversions [being applied](https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/master/opencensus/trace/span.py#L222) to all spans (such as string truncation) that may not necessarily apply to all export formats.

I am proposing to update the [base.Exporter](https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/master/opencensus/trace/exporters/base.py#L18) emit and export methods to take a raw [span.Span](https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/master/opencensus/trace/span.py#L28) object instead of a dict. Not only will this push stackdriver specific details to the stackdriver exporter, but it would also make the development of new exporters easier since there are less layers of conversion.

Happy to make a PR if everyone is ok with the change.

issue_title: JVM Access Violation when calling python module with numpy
Support sending spans over HTTP
issue_body:
Similar to the [HttpSender](https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-java/blob/master/jaeger-core/src/main/java/com/uber/jaeger/senders/HttpSender.java) in jaeger-java. It seems like it could be as simple as using THttpClient in place of TUDPTransport and  connecting to the collector directly. I'm happy to help out with the implementation if needed.

Background: I have some applications that are generating huge spans (> 65k) because they are including full SQL and ES queries as logs in the trace. Right now we are manually truncating the queries, but my clients would like to be able to view them for debugging purposes.

issue_title: Change Blurr Transform Spec YAML Parsing for better error messages
issue_body:
#### Issue Checklist:
- [x] Descriptive title
- [x] Completed sections below
- [ ] Bug report? Relevant debug logs / reproduction information

## Problem Description
PyYaml throws cryptic errors for YAML parsing errors.  The developers designing Blurr Transform Schema often struggle to determine where the error is occurring within the YAML document.


## Solution Proposal
We can check out other YAML parsers such as [Ruamel](http://yaml.readthedocs.io/en/latest/), or see how exceptions raised by PyYAML be trapped and more readable errors messages are returned.


## Impact on current behavior
None.  It should however improve the readabililty of errors

issue_title: ListField with ForeignKeys
issue_body:
Nearly works with Mongo (somewhat similar to issue 47 there), might be expressive in some cases.

```python
def test_list_with_foreignkeys(self):
    class Model(models.Model):
        pass
    class ReferenceList(models.Model):
        keys = ListField(models.ForeignKey(Model))
    model1 = Model.objects.create()
    model2 = Model.objects.create()
    ReferenceList.objects.create(keys=[model1, model2])
    self.assertEqual(
        Model.objects.get(pk=ReferenceList.objects.all()[0].keys[0]),
        model1)
```
